title: thymus quinquecostatus var. magnus
intro: Thymus quinquecostatus var. magnus, also known as the large five-angled thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal effects. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or essential oil, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in herbal remedies.
